dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4217 IS: Copay Fairness for Veterans Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Duckworth (for herself and Ms. Collins) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to eliminate copayments by the Department of Veterans Affairs for preventive health services and medicines relating to preventive health services, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Copay Fairness for Veterans Act of 2026.

section.header: Improvement to preventive health services furnished by Department of Veterans Affairs

paragraph.text: Section 1722A(a)(4) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking to opioid antagonists and inserting “to—
by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:
medication, including over-the-counter medication, that is or is part of preventive health services.

paragraph.text: Section 1710 of such title is amended—

clause.text: by redesignating paragraph (5) as paragraph (6); and
by inserting after paragraph (4) the following new paragraph (5):

paragraph.text: A veteran shall not be liable to the United States under this subsection for any amounts for preventive health services the veteran receives during the course of hospital care or nursing home care provided to the veteran.

subparagraph.text: in subsection (g)(3), by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

paragraph.text: Section 1725A(f)(1) of such title is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: An eligible veteran shall not be required to pay the United States a copayment for preventive health services furnished under this section.

paragraph.text: Section 1781 of such title is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.text: No individual covered under subsection (a) shall be required to pay the United States a copayment for preventive health services furnished under this section.
Section 1701(9) of such title is amended—

paragraph.text: by amending subparagraph (G) to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: immunizations against infectious diseases, including each immunization—

clause.text: on the recommended adult immunization schedule at the time such immunization is indicated on that schedule;

that has in effect a recommendation from the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention with respect to the individual receiving the immunization; or

that is recommended by the largest single-discipline professional organization for the relevant field or a major labor or professional organization that exclusively represents the relevant clinical professional, specialty, or disorder;

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (K), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
by redesignating subparagraph (L) as subparagraph (N); and
by inserting after subparagraph (K) the following new subparagraphs:

clause.text: have in effect a rating of A or B in the current recommendations of the United States Preventive Services Task Force; or

are recommended by the largest single-discipline professional organization for the relevant field or a major labor or professional organization that exclusively represents the relevant clinical professional, specialty, or disorder;

subparagraph.text: with respect to preventive care, screenings, and contraceptive services, such services shall include, at minimum—

clause.text: screening for anxiety, breast cancer, cervical cancer, human immunodeficiency virus infection, intimate partner and domestic violence, diabetes in and after pregnancy, and urinary incontinence;
counseling for intimate partner and domestic violence, and sexually transmitted infections;

subclause.text: any drug, device, or biological product intended for use in the prevention of pregnancy, whether specifically intended to prevent pregnancy or for other health needs, that is approved, cleared, authorized, or licensed under section 505, 510(k), 513(f)(2), 515, or 564 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355, 360(k), 360c(f)(2), 360e, 360bbb–3) or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 262);
any services related to providing the safe and effective use of such drug, device, or product; and
any related services approved, granted, or cleared by the Food and Drug Administration;

clause.text: obesity prevention in midlife women; and

subsection.text: Nothing in this section or the amendments made by this section shall be construed to—

paragraph.text: remove preventive health services, including medications, from such services and medications for which a veteran would be exempt from paying the United States a copayment; or

prevent coverage under the laws administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs of preventive health services, screenings, and contraceptive services, if such preventive health services, screenings, and contraceptive services are provided in accordance with evidence-based medical standards of care, which may include those recommendations provided for in the Preventive Services Guidelines of the Health Resources and Services Administration.

subsection.text: This section and the amendments made by this section shall take effect on the date that is 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.
